terms.definition: A concise expression of the form [expr for item in iterable if cond] that builds a new list from an iterable.
An expression of the form {key: value for item in iterable} that builds a dictionary in one statement.
An expression of the form {expr for item in iterable} that builds a set of unique values.
A comprehension written with parentheses, e.g. (x for x in xs), that yields items lazily on demand instead of building a full collection in memory.
The optional trailing `if condition` in a comprehension that keeps only items for which the condition is truthy.
An inline `a if condition else b` used in the value slot of a comprehension to choose a value per item.
